  • Method Details

    • account

      public CalculateCashBalanceResponse.Builder account(String account)
      The account.

    • account

      public CalculateCashBalanceResponse.Builder account(Optional<String> account)
      The account.

    • availableCashToWithdrawAmount

      public CalculateCashBalanceResponse.Builder availableCashToWithdrawAmount(AvailableCashToWithdrawAmount availableCashToWithdrawAmount)
      The account's available cash to withdraw in USD. It is calculated based on the `open_balance_amount` and account activity. This value is always non-negative. If `unadjusted_available_cash_to_withdraw_amount` is negative, this value will be `0`.

    • availableCashToWithdrawAmount

      public CalculateCashBalanceResponse.Builder availableCashToWithdrawAmount(org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends AvailableCashToWithdrawAmount> availableCashToWithdrawAmount)
      The account's available cash to withdraw in USD. It is calculated based on the `open_balance_amount` and account activity. This value is always non-negative. If `unadjusted_available_cash_to_withdraw_amount` is negative, this value will be `0`.

    • availableLiquidityAmount

      public CalculateCashBalanceResponse.Builder availableLiquidityAmount(AvailableLiquidityAmount availableLiquidityAmount)
      The account's available liquidity in USD including the value of cash and equity positions. It is calculated based on the `open_liquidity_amount` and account activity. This value can be negative.

    • availableLiquidityAmount

      public CalculateCashBalanceResponse.Builder availableLiquidityAmount(org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends AvailableLiquidityAmount> availableLiquidityAmount)
      The account's available liquidity in USD including the value of cash and equity positions. It is calculated based on the `open_liquidity_amount` and account activity. This value can be negative.

    • fullyPaidUnsettledFundsAmount

      public CalculateCashBalanceResponse.Builder fullyPaidUnsettledFundsAmount(FullyPaidUnsettledFundsAmount fullyPaidUnsettledFundsAmount)
      The account's fully paid unsettled funds in USD. Fully paid unsettled funds are proceeds from sales of assets paid for with cash and not bought on margin. If `include_fully_paid_unsettled_funds` is `false`, this value is **subtracted** from `open_balance_amount` and `open_liquidity_amount` to calculate `available_cash_to_withdraw_amount` and `available_liquidity_amount`. This value is always non-negative.

    • fullyPaidUnsettledFundsAmount

      public CalculateCashBalanceResponse.Builder fullyPaidUnsettledFundsAmount(org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends FullyPaidUnsettledFundsAmount> fullyPaidUnsettledFundsAmount)
      The account's fully paid unsettled funds in USD. Fully paid unsettled funds are proceeds from sales of assets paid for with cash and not bought on margin. If `include_fully_paid_unsettled_funds` is `false`, this value is **subtracted** from `open_balance_amount` and `open_liquidity_amount` to calculate `available_cash_to_withdraw_amount` and `available_liquidity_amount`. This value is always non-negative.

    • includeFullyPaidUnsettledFunds

      public CalculateCashBalanceResponse.Builder includeFullyPaidUnsettledFunds(boolean includeFullyPaidUnsettledFunds)
      Whether fully paid unsettled funds are included in balances.

    • includeFullyPaidUnsettledFunds

      public CalculateCashBalanceResponse.Builder includeFullyPaidUnsettledFunds(Optional<Boolean> includeFullyPaidUnsettledFunds)
      Whether fully paid unsettled funds are included in balances.

    • intradayBuyTrades

      public CalculateCashBalanceResponse.Builder intradayBuyTrades(List<CalculateCashBalanceResponseTradeSummary> intradayBuyTrades)
      The account's intraday buy trades. The net amounts are **subtracted** from `open_balance_amount` and `open_liquidity_amount` to calculate `available_cash_to_withdraw_amount` and `available_liquidity_amount`. Since trades have positive amounts, the calculated values will **decrease**.

    • intradayBuyTrades

      public CalculateCashBalanceResponse.Builder intradayBuyTrades(Optional<? extends List<CalculateCashBalanceResponseTradeSummary>> intradayBuyTrades)
      The account's intraday buy trades. The net amounts are **subtracted** from `open_balance_amount` and `open_liquidity_amount` to calculate `available_cash_to_withdraw_amount` and `available_liquidity_amount`. Since trades have positive amounts, the calculated values will **decrease**.

    • intradayDeposits

      public CalculateCashBalanceResponse.Builder intradayDeposits(List<CalculateCashBalanceResponseTransferSummary> intradayDeposits)
      The account's eligible intraday deposits that have posted to the ledger. Intraday deposits may be eligible based on mechanism (e.g. intraday ICT deposits are eligible for ICT withdrawals, but not ACH withdrawals). The amounts are **added** to `open_balance_amount` and `open_liquidity_amount` to calculate `available_cash_to_withdraw_amount` and `available_liquidity_amount`. Since deposits have positive amounts, the calculated values will **increase**.

    • intradayDeposits

      public CalculateCashBalanceResponse.Builder intradayDeposits(Optional<? extends List<CalculateCashBalanceResponseTransferSummary>> intradayDeposits)
      The account's eligible intraday deposits that have posted to the ledger. Intraday deposits may be eligible based on mechanism (e.g. intraday ICT deposits are eligible for ICT withdrawals, but not ACH withdrawals). The amounts are **added** to `open_balance_amount` and `open_liquidity_amount` to calculate `available_cash_to_withdraw_amount` and `available_liquidity_amount`. Since deposits have positive amounts, the calculated values will **increase**.

    • intradayReturns

      public CalculateCashBalanceResponse.Builder intradayReturns(List<CalculateCashBalanceResponseTransferSummary> intradayReturns)
      The account's intraday returns. The amounts are **subtracted** from `open_balance_amount` and `open_liquidity_amount` to calculate `available_cash_to_withdraw_amount` and `available_liquidity_amount`. Returned deposits will **decrease** calculated values because they have positive amounts and returned withdrawals will **increase** calculated values because they have negative amounts.

    • intradayReturns

      public CalculateCashBalanceResponse.Builder intradayReturns(Optional<? extends List<CalculateCashBalanceResponseTransferSummary>> intradayReturns)
      The account's intraday returns. The amounts are **subtracted** from `open_balance_amount` and `open_liquidity_amount` to calculate `available_cash_to_withdraw_amount` and `available_liquidity_amount`. Returned deposits will **decrease** calculated values because they have positive amounts and returned withdrawals will **increase** calculated values because they have negative amounts.

    • intradayWithdrawals

      public CalculateCashBalanceResponse.Builder intradayWithdrawals(List<CalculateCashBalanceResponseTransferSummary> intradayWithdrawals)
      The account's intraday withdrawals that have posted to the ledger. The amounts are **added** to `open_balance_amount` and `open_liquidity_amount` to calculate `available_cash_to_withdraw_amount` and `available_liquidity_amount`. Since withdrawals have negative amounts, the calculated values will **decrease**.

    • intradayWithdrawals

      public CalculateCashBalanceResponse.Builder intradayWithdrawals(Optional<? extends List<CalculateCashBalanceResponseTransferSummary>> intradayWithdrawals)
      The account's intraday withdrawals that have posted to the ledger. The amounts are **added** to `open_balance_amount` and `open_liquidity_amount` to calculate `available_cash_to_withdraw_amount` and `available_liquidity_amount`. Since withdrawals have negative amounts, the calculated values will **decrease**.

    • openBalanceAmount

      public CalculateCashBalanceResponse.Builder openBalanceAmount(OpenBalanceAmount openBalanceAmount)
      The account's starting cash balance for the day in USD as calculated by the margin system. It is used as the base to calculate `available_cash_to_withdraw_amount`. This value is always non-negative.

    • openBalanceAmount

      public CalculateCashBalanceResponse.Builder openBalanceAmount(org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends OpenBalanceAmount> openBalanceAmount)
      The account's starting cash balance for the day in USD as calculated by the margin system. It is used as the base to calculate `available_cash_to_withdraw_amount`. This value is always non-negative.

    • openBuyOrders

      public CalculateCashBalanceResponse.Builder openBuyOrders(List<CalculateCashBalanceResponseOpenOrderSummary> openBuyOrders)
      The account's open buy orders. The expected notional ceilings are **subtracted** from `open_balance_amount` and `open_liquidity_amount` to calculate `available_cash_to_withdraw_amount` and `available_liquidity_amount`. Since open orders have positive expected notional ceilings, the calculated values will **decrease**.

    • openBuyOrders

      public CalculateCashBalanceResponse.Builder openBuyOrders(Optional<? extends List<CalculateCashBalanceResponseOpenOrderSummary>> openBuyOrders)
      The account's open buy orders. The expected notional ceilings are **subtracted** from `open_balance_amount` and `open_liquidity_amount` to calculate `available_cash_to_withdraw_amount` and `available_liquidity_amount`. Since open orders have positive expected notional ceilings, the calculated values will **decrease**.

    • openLiquidityAmount

      public CalculateCashBalanceResponse.Builder openLiquidityAmount(OpenLiquidityAmount openLiquidityAmount)
      The account's starting liquidity for the day in USD as calculated by the margin system. It includes the value of all asset positions (e.g. cash, equities, fixed income, etc). It is used as the base to calculate `available_liquidity_amount`. This value can be negative.

    • openLiquidityAmount

      public CalculateCashBalanceResponse.Builder openLiquidityAmount(org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends OpenLiquidityAmount> openLiquidityAmount)
      The account's starting liquidity for the day in USD as calculated by the margin system. It includes the value of all asset positions (e.g. cash, equities, fixed income, etc). It is used as the base to calculate `available_liquidity_amount`. This value can be negative.

    • pendingDebitDividendsAmount

      public CalculateCashBalanceResponse.Builder pendingDebitDividendsAmount(PendingDebitDividendsAmount pendingDebitDividendsAmount)
      The account's pending debit dividends in USD. The value is **subtracted** from `open_balance_amount` and `open_liquidity_amount` to calculate `available_cash_to_withdraw_amount` and `available_liquidity_amount`. This value is always non-negative.

    • pendingDebitDividendsAmount

      public CalculateCashBalanceResponse.Builder pendingDebitDividendsAmount(org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends PendingDebitDividendsAmount> pendingDebitDividendsAmount)
      The account's pending debit dividends in USD. The value is **subtracted** from `open_balance_amount` and `open_liquidity_amount` to calculate `available_cash_to_withdraw_amount` and `available_liquidity_amount`. This value is always non-negative.

    • pendingDebitInterestAmount

      public CalculateCashBalanceResponse.Builder pendingDebitInterestAmount(PendingDebitInterestAmount pendingDebitInterestAmount)
      The account's pending debit interest in USD. The value is **subtracted** from `open_balance_amount` and `open_liquidity_amount` to calculate `available_cash_to_withdraw_amount` and `available_liquidity_amount`. This value is always non-negative.

    • pendingDebitInterestAmount

      public CalculateCashBalanceResponse.Builder pendingDebitInterestAmount(org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends PendingDebitInterestAmount> pendingDebitInterestAmount)
      The account's pending debit interest in USD. The value is **subtracted** from `open_balance_amount` and `open_liquidity_amount` to calculate `available_cash_to_withdraw_amount` and `available_liquidity_amount`. This value is always non-negative.

    • pendingWithdrawals

      public CalculateCashBalanceResponse.Builder pendingWithdrawals(List<CalculateCashBalanceResponseTransferSummary> pendingWithdrawals)
      The account's pending withdrawals. Pending withdrawals may need review and have yet to post to the ledger. The amounts are **added** to `open_balance_amount` and `open_liquidity_amount` to calculate `available_cash_to_withdraw_amount` and `available_liquidity_amount`. Since withdrawals have negative amounts, the calculated values will **decrease**.

    • pendingWithdrawals

      public CalculateCashBalanceResponse.Builder pendingWithdrawals(Optional<? extends List<CalculateCashBalanceResponseTransferSummary>> pendingWithdrawals)
      The account's pending withdrawals. Pending withdrawals may need review and have yet to post to the ledger. The amounts are **added** to `open_balance_amount` and `open_liquidity_amount` to calculate `available_cash_to_withdraw_amount` and `available_liquidity_amount`. Since withdrawals have negative amounts, the calculated values will **decrease**.

    • unadjustedAvailableCashToWithdrawAmount

      public CalculateCashBalanceResponse.Builder unadjustedAvailableCashToWithdrawAmount(UnadjustedAvailableCashToWithdrawAmount unadjustedAvailableCashToWithdrawAmount)
      The account's unadjusted available cash to withdraw in USD. It is calculated based on the `open_balance_amount` and account activity. This value can be negative.

    • unadjustedAvailableCashToWithdrawAmount

      public CalculateCashBalanceResponse.Builder unadjustedAvailableCashToWithdrawAmount(org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends UnadjustedAvailableCashToWithdrawAmount> unadjustedAvailableCashToWithdrawAmount)
      The account's unadjusted available cash to withdraw in USD. It is calculated based on the `open_balance_amount` and account activity. This value can be negative.

    • withheldDepositThresholdAmount

      public CalculateCashBalanceResponse.Builder withheldDepositThresholdAmount(WithheldDepositThresholdAmount withheldDepositThresholdAmount)
      The account's withheld deposit threshold. Amounts in `withheld_deposits` up to this threshold will be not be subtracted to calculate `available_cash_to_withdraw_amount` and `available_liquidity_amount`. This value is always non-negative.

    • withheldDepositThresholdAmount

      public CalculateCashBalanceResponse.Builder withheldDepositThresholdAmount(org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends WithheldDepositThresholdAmount> withheldDepositThresholdAmount)
      The account's withheld deposit threshold. Amounts in `withheld_deposits` up to this threshold will be not be subtracted to calculate `available_cash_to_withdraw_amount` and `available_liquidity_amount`. This value is always non-negative.

    • withheldDeposits

      public CalculateCashBalanceResponse.Builder withheldDeposits(List<CalculateCashBalanceResponseTransferSummary> withheldDeposits)
      The account's withheld deposits. Deposits are withheld if there is a risk of a return (e.g. ACH deposits). Typically, they are withheld for 5 business days after completing. The amounts are **subtracted** from `open_balance_amount` and `open_liquidity_amount` to calculate `available_cash_to_withdraw_amount` and `available_liquidity_amount`. Since deposits have positive amounts, the calculated values will **decrease**. However, withheld deposits will not be subtracted up to the `withheld_deposit_threshold_amount`.

    • withheldDeposits

      public CalculateCashBalanceResponse.Builder withheldDeposits(Optional<? extends List<CalculateCashBalanceResponseTransferSummary>> withheldDeposits)
      The account's withheld deposits. Deposits are withheld if there is a risk of a return (e.g. ACH deposits). Typically, they are withheld for 5 business days after completing. The amounts are **subtracted** from `open_balance_amount` and `open_liquidity_amount` to calculate `available_cash_to_withdraw_amount` and `available_liquidity_amount`. Since deposits have positive amounts, the calculated values will **decrease**. However, withheld deposits will not be subtracted up to the `withheld_deposit_threshold_amount`.

    • build

      public CalculateCashBalanceResponse build()
